math.u[n]: Discrete-time unit step function / Heaviside step function.
math.h[n]: Impulse response of an LTI discrete-time system.
math.h(t): Impulse response of an LTI continuous-time system.
math.X(f): Frequency domain representation (Fourier transform) of x(t).
math.W_{kn}: Twiddle factor e^{-2\pi i n k / N}.
math.P_x(f_k): Periodogram (estimate of the signal's power distribution).
math.s = \\sigma + i\\omega: Complex frequency in the Laplace domain.
math.H(z): Discrete-time transfer function in the z-domain.
math.z: Complex variable in the z-domain (z = e^{sT}).
math.c_{xy}(\\tau): Cross correlation between x and y.
math.\\hat{x}(t): Continuous representation of the sampled signal.
math.\\int t^k \\psi(t) dt = 0: Condition for a wavelet to have n vanishing moments.
math.N: Number of points in a Discrete Fourier Transform.

text.Temporal signal: A signal where the independent variable is time (e.g. voltage).
text.Spatial signal: A signal where the independent variable is space (e.g. image).
text.Continuous-time signal: Signal defined for a continuous range of time.
text.Discrete-time signal: Signal defined only at discrete time instances.
text.Analog signal: Signal taking amplitude values from a continuous range.
text.Digital signal: Signal taking amplitude values from a discrete set.
text.System: A process where an input signal is transformed into an output signal.
text.Continuous-time system: Transforms a continuous-time input into a continuous-time output.
text.Discrete-time system: Transforms a discrete-time input into a discrete-time output.
text.Signal processing: Acquisition, representation, manipulation, and transformation of signals.
text.Absolutely summable: A sequence whose absolute values sum to a finite number.
text.Square summable: A sequence whose squared absolute values sum to a finite number (Energy signal).
text.Power signal: A non-square-summable sequence with a finite average power.
text.Causal system: System whose output depends only on present and past input values.
text.Memory-less system: System whose output depends only on the current input value.
text.Time-invariant system: System where a time shift in input causes an identical time shift in output.
text.Stable system: System that is bounded-input bounded-output (BIBO) stable.
text.Convolution sum: Mathematical operation calculating the output of a discrete LTI system.
text.Impulse response: The output of an LTI system when the input is a unit impulse.
text.Fourier series: Expansion of a periodic signal into a sum of sines and cosines.
text.Dirac comb: An infinite series of Dirac delta functions spaced at equal intervals.
text.Aliasing: Phenomenon where distinct continuous signals produce identical discrete sample sequences.
text.Nyquist-Shannon sampling theorem: Theorem stating a bandlimited signal can be perfectly reconstructed if sampled at more than twice its highest frequency.
text.Nyquist rate: The minimum sampling rate required to avoid aliasing.
text.Antialiasing filter: A low-pass filter applied before sampling to remove frequencies above the Nyquist limit.
text.Cooley-Tukey algorithm: Algorithm for Fast Fourier Transform reducing operation count to O(N log_2 N).
text.Zero padding: Extending sequence lengths with zeros to interpolate the frequency spectrum.
text.Periodogram: An estimate of the signal's power distribution over frequency.
text.Power spectral density: The limiting value of the periodogram over an infinitely long time interval.
text.Bartlett method: Method of estimating PSD by subdividing data into non-overlapping segments and averaging their periodograms.
text.Welch's method: Method of estimating PSD using overlapping, windowed segments.
text.Spectral leakage: Energy spreading due to convolution with the Fourier transform of the window function.
text.Scalloping loss: Amplitude underestimation caused by sampling a continuous spectrum at discrete FFT bin frequencies.
text.Bilinear transform: A method used to discretize continuous-time filters into discrete-time filters.
text.Pre-warping: Modifying the s-domain transfer function so that the digital filter's frequency of interest aligns with the analog one.
text.Butterworth filter: Filter with no ripples, monotonically falling gain.
text.Chebyshev filter: Filter that distributes the gain error uniformly throughout the passband or stopband (equiripples).
text.Short time Fourier transform (STFT): Fourier transform of a signal multiplied by a sliding window, used for temporal localization.
text.Continuous wavelet transform (CWT): Transform using scaled and translated wavelets.
text.White noise: Noise with equal power at every frequency.
text.Pink noise: Noise with power inversely proportional to frequency (1/f noise).
text.Time delay embedding: Method to reconstruct dynamics from an observation by creating a multivariate time series using delayed versions.
text.Phase space: Space in which all possible states of a system are represented.
text.Phase coherence: Measure that characterizes the stability of the phase differences between two signals.
text.Wiener-Khinchin theorem: Relates the power spectral density to the Fourier transform of the autocorrelation function.
text.Attractor: Set of states toward which a dynamical system tends to evolve over time.
